purpose: IF-10 cross-module const-gated dead branch — GO port (§C5). Ports the resolve-to-literal substrate (TS §29 / Python §30) to a corpus language. Go's package-qualified references (pkg.NAME) make the cross-module edge explicit, so the fold resolves a const literal across the package boundary and grounds the dead arm to the origin's file:line. The detector must fire ONLY on a const-literal resolved across a DIFFERENT package, never on a var (non-literal) or a bare intra-package reference.

expected_issues.claim: Dead branch: flags.NewCheckout is const false, so the if-body is unreachable.
Dead branch: flags.LegacyMode is const true, so !flags.LegacyMode is always false and the if-body is unreachable.

must_not_fire.reason: config.DynamicFlag is a VAR (runtime-assigned via computeFlag()), not a const-literal — the value is not statically known, so there is no decidable dead arm; suppress.
NewCheckout here is a BARE same-package reference (not flags.NewCheckout) — intra-component, §25 in-file territory, not a cross-module fire; suppress.
